name: mimesis-reference-pack-index
purpose: Machine-readable source-first standards index for choosing reference packs before transforming one weak artifact.

packs.title: AI Workflow Reference Pack
packs.useWhen: Use this when an AI workflow produces output but no durable artifact trail.
packs.sourceQuality:
Prefer official tool docs, source repos, protocol specs, papers, standards, and maintained examples with explicit inputs and outputs.
Avoid hidden prompts, private evaluations, and unverified automation claims.
packs.starterPrompt:
Study the reference AI workflows below.
Extract their structure, not their wording.

Identify input artifact, AI role, constraints, verification, memory, output artifact, and failure handling.

Then transform my workflow so it leaves an artifact trail and proof boundary.

packs.title: Blog Post Reference Pack
packs.useWhen: Use this when a draft has an idea but no strong reading path.
packs.sourceQuality:
Prefer original essays, first-party engineering posts, papers, standards, source repos, and public talks with stable links.
Avoid derivative summaries when the original source is available.
doNotCopy: claims that belong to the reference author
packs.starterPrompt:
Study the reference posts below.
Extract their structure, not their voice.

Identify thesis, tension, narrative arc, examples, insight density, proof boundaries, and ending CTA.

Then transform my draft while preserving my own evidence and constraints.

packs.title: GitHub README Reference Pack
packs.useWhen: You want to make a GitHub repo easier to understand, trust, use, and contribute to.
packs.sourceQuality:
Prefer official project repositories, maintained source repos, release docs, contribution guides, and public issue or discussion trails.
Avoid using marketing summaries as proof of how a README actually works.
packs.starterPrompt:
Study the reference READMEs below.
Extract their structure, not their wording.

Identify how they explain the problem, show the first action, build trust, state limitations, and invite contribution.

Then transform my README under my project's constraints.

packs.title: Landing Page Reference Pack
packs.useWhen: Use this when a landing page feels generic, unclear, overdesigned, or unearned.
packs.sourceQuality:
Prefer official product pages, official docs, docs-linked pages, public changelogs, pricing pages, and case pages with visible proof boundaries.
Avoid swipe files, screenshots without permission, and summaries that hide the original page.
doNotCopy: customer proof you do not have
packs.starterPrompt:
Study the reference landing pages below.
Extract structure, not surface.

Identify their hero clarity, pain statement, transformation promise, proof rhythm, CTA path, objection handling, and claim boundary.

Then transform my landing page under my audience, proof, and product constraints.
Do not invent customer proof.

packs.title: Product Page Reference Pack
packs.useWhen: Use this when a product page explains features but does not make the use case feel concrete.
packs.sourceQuality:
Prefer official product pages, maintained docs, public demos, release notes, source repos, and help-center flows.
Avoid copying customer logos, pricing claims, or product screenshots you do not have permission to use.
packs.starterPrompt:
Study the reference product pages below.
Extract their structure, not their claims.

Identify the audience, use case, workflow, proof rhythm, comparison frame, trust devices, and activation path.

Then transform my product page under my actual product, audience, evidence, and constraints.

packs.title: Reference Pack: Profile Positioning
packs.useWhen:
Use this when a GitHub profile, LinkedIn profile, personal homepage, or creator bio does not yet route attention to the right proof surface.

This pack is informed by the neighboring `mimesis-casebook` profile cases.
packs.sourceQuality:
Prefer local artifacts, public profiles, official project pages, source repos, case notes, and proof pages with visible claim boundaries.
Avoid private analytics, copied bios, and unverifiable social proof.
transferablePatterns: distribution surface separate from source of truth
doNotCopy: authority signals you have not earned
packs.starterPrompt:
Study these profile and positioning artifacts as standards.

Identify the thesis, proof surface, routing path, stale claims, and claim boundary.

Then transform my profile under my actual proof and audience constraints.
Do not invent external validation.

packs.title: Reference Pack: Research Note
packs.useWhen: Use this when a research note sounds smart but does not yet show source quality, method limits, or proof boundaries.
packs.sourceQuality:
Prefer papers, standards, patents, official docs, maintained source repos, datasets, and directly inspectable benchmark setups.
Use secondary commentary only to route back to the primary source.
doNotCopy: benchmark numbers without source and setup
legal, medical, financial, or safety claims without qualified review
packs.starterPrompt:
Study these primary sources and maintained implementations as standards.

Identify the problem statement, source quality, claim hierarchy, method comparison, evidence chain, limitations, and next experiment.

Then transform my research note under my actual evidence boundary.
Do not turn weak evidence into a strong claim.

boundary: does not permit copying protected surface
